title: AI Value Comes From Workflow Redesign and Governance, Not Tools Alone
intent: Capture the shift from AI experimentation to operating-model rewiring.

source.title: The state of AI: How organizations are rewiring to capture value
source.methodology: McKinsey Global Survey on AI; 1,491 participants in 101 nations.

fact.primary_stat: 78% of respondents said their organizations use AI in at least one business function.
supporting_stats: 71% said their organizations regularly use generative AI in at least one business function.
More than 80% said their organizations are not yet seeing tangible enterprise-level EBIT impact from gen AI.
Less than one-third reported that their organizations follow most of McKinsey’s listed adoption and scaling practices for gen AI.

business_relevance: Enterprise AI maturity is no longer just experimentation; scale is outpacing disciplined operating-model change.
Value capture is constrained when adoption practices lag behind tool deployment.
dzmitryi_commentary: This is probably the most important signal for my focus. In support-heavy SAP landscapes, real AI value does not come from a chatbot bolted onto the side. It comes when triage, knowledge capture, handover, escalation, and prevention loops are redesigned so the model fits the process instead of bypassing it.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Capture the shift from AI experimentation to operating-model rewiring.

attribution.preferred_citation: Dzmitryi Kharlanau. “AI Value Comes From Workflow Redesign and Governance, Not Tools Alone” (dataset bytes). CC BY-NC 4.0. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18862098. https://dkharlanau.github.io/datasets/ai-business-signals/aibs-008.json
